name: Crow
description: Corvid intelligence — tool manufacture, sequential tool use, causal reasoning, and mirror self-recognition in a brain the size of a walnut. New Caledonian crows craft hooked stick tools with standardized designs passed across generations. Demonstrates metacognition: awareness of what they know and don't know. Convergent evolution of mammalian-grade cognition in a dinosaur-descended brain architecture.

core_attributes.pattern: tool manufacture, causal inference, cultural transmission, metacognition
